ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Hɛrɔd funɛ sou, Koosa malaika ti fun bur wa Josɛf wati a li Ijipt pulum ni.
translated: An angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and told him to go to Egypt.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Koosa la nyɛ kɔsa wa kilaarɛ. A wa bi a vusi ka limaŋ wa pisi, din yaa wa di a. O wati ma kaali kɔsa kinkan. O wa zi bu koti a nyɛ ma kilaarɛɛ? Ma se wa su un nɛnɛ.
translated: The chief's wife is a troublesome woman. She doesn't farm and doesn't take care of the children, yet she eats. She went to call the woman a witch. Will she not bewitch her that she becomes ill? I won't say it again.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Doone o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun wa pɛɛsi un, “I funɛ biksi nara, baŋnɛ i fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a nyɛ ba?”
translated: The chief and the elders called him to come and stand before them, “You have done a wonderful thing, how did you manage to do it like that?”

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Lɔsa funɛ kpak min, ma wa fun nyɛ min diwe mu di. Nyɔksa funɛ kpak min, ma wa fun nyɛ min ni mu nyɔ.
translated: When the rain rains on the farm, we will be happy to farm on it. When the sun shines on the farm, we will be happy to dry (things) on it.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Lalaknɔ ba naa maga nyɛn Galili pulum ni fun nu chenani o tum. Yesu Fun Tiibi Nidamata (Mat.
translated: And when they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret. The people of that place recognized him

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta bɔnɛ funɛ nyɛn dooni a lisi ba tusa ni, “O sɛm sɛma nan ni, o kpa o titirɛ a chiŋsi Koosa bar chiŋi ni.”
translated: The dog's mother and father were very happy and said to their children, “You should go and look for food at Koosa's house.”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Yesu fun burgi a pɛɛsi ba, “Nar kpa haan ka o chɛnsa tusa bu chɔkuu? Aai. Ba wa zi ya chen. Saha naa ba zi wa kpak baal ti a kaali, chen wuzu ba zi wa vɔu nɔnau.”
translated: “Why did you doubt me, Thomas? You have seen me and still you doubt. Blessed are those who have not seen and yet believe. They will be given many blessings and a special place in heaven.”

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: O wa zi vusi ka ma damsa wa chɔk.
translated: The dog chased the goat.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma nyɛ mu ŋɔ ma bar nɔ wɛ. Saha zi wa wau, nara zi wa chɔk kina maga a li bar nɔ ni. Ba wa zi wa gil buŋkul bɛgim o sak o dɔŋtiin ni.” Tɛɛnɛ Nɔ Siili Saha Namsi (Maak
translated: The man said, “You are the one I have been looking for. If you are a person who has a strong heart, then let us go and see who will be the one to kill the other. I will show you my power and my strength.”

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Nihaana ba naa funɛ tiŋ un a la suŋi un a li Galili ni chen, gbanko fun a chiŋi bar boli ni a nyini chenani funɛ ya.
translated: The people were talking to each other about what Jesus had done, and a Pharisee and a lawyer asked him, "Why do you not follow the traditions of our ancestors, but instead eat with sinners?"
